title: EvaluateBatchRequest
description: Wire payload sent to POST /v2/evaluate:batch. Pillar 2 of V2: one HTTP call for N decisions, one rate-limit decrement for the whole batch, one hash-chain entry listing every decision id. Ordering is preserved — responses[i] is the decision for requests[i].

requests.description: Per-item evaluate requests. Upper bound (1000) matches the v1 rate-limit bucket size; raising it means revisiting per-key budgets. Open question: should items share a single api_key (implied: they do, from the envelope) or allow per-item overrides? Recommendation: no — one key per batch keeps auth simple and avoids fan-out across orgs.

api_key.description: API key echoed in the body, same semantics as /v1-evaluate's api_key field.

payload_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hex of canonicalJSON(payload), for batches that also opt into the Pillar 9 proof flow. When present on a batch item, the corresponding response item carries proof_id and proof_status for later consume.

target.description: Optional target resource identifier. Mirrors Proof.target when the item opts into the Pillar 9 proof flow.
